title: Map begin, resume, and active-transaction selection code paths
goal: Review the current runtime implementation and identify every code path involved in starting a transaction, resuming a transaction, selecting the active transaction, and deciding whether new work may begin.
why_this_ticket_exists: The 0.6.0 redesign depends on exact active-transaction continuation, structural no-active handling, and elimination of heuristic recovery behavior. Before changing runtime behavior, the code paths responsible for begin and resume decisions must be mapped precisely so later tickets can target the correct files and functions without drifting from the plan.
scope: Review all Python modules under src that participate in transaction creation, transaction lookup, active transaction selection, resume decision logic, lifecycle continuation, or no-active handling.
Identify entrypoints that decide whether to start new work or resume existing work.
Identify all code paths that read materialized transaction state before making a begin or resume decision.
Identify all code paths that inspect event history, session context, timestamps, sentinel values, or string patterns during begin or resume decisions.
Identify all code paths that determine active transaction identity, active ticket identity, or resumability.
Identify any helper or wrapper APIs that can indirectly influence begin or resume behavior.
out_of_scope: Implementing new ticket or transaction semantics.
work_products: A file-by-file map of all Python modules relevant to begin and resume behavior.
A function-level map of transaction creation, transaction resume, and active-transaction selection logic.
A list of places where heuristics are currently used or may be used, including string-shape assumptions, sentinel IDs, timestamp-first selection, or session-driven selection.
A list of the files most likely to be modified by later identity, resume, and no-active-state tickets.
A short summary of risks or ambiguities in the current code paths.

expected_outputs.name: begin_resume_codepath_map
expected_outputs.description: Human-readable map of Python files, functions, and responsibilities related to begin and resume behavior.

expected_outputs.name: resume_risk_inventory
expected_outputs.description: List of heuristic, ambiguous, or legacy-sensitive code paths that may conflict with the 0.6.0 protocol.

expected_outputs.name: downstream_impact_list
expected_outputs.description: List of files and modules likely to be touched by later tickets such as issued tx_id adoption, structural no-active handling, and next_action-driven resume.

acceptance_criteria: All Python files under src that are relevant to begin, resume, or active-transaction selection have been reviewed.
The output identifies the exact functions or classes that create a transaction, resume a transaction, or select the active transaction.
The output identifies whether active transaction selection depends on exact identity, sentinel handling, timestamps, session context, or string heuristics.
The output makes it easy for later tickets to determine which files should be modified for identity changes, no-active representation changes, and next_action-driven resume changes.
The mapping remains descriptive only and does not change runtime behavior.
notes: Use the plan definitions, not current implementation habits, as the evaluation baseline.
Pay special attention to any place where resume behavior depends on data outside the minimal canonical continuation contract.
Pay special attention to any place where no-active state is represented by sentinel values or reconstructed heuristically.
The output of this ticket should be referenced by later implementation tickets when selecting target files.
